title: Formulas within dashboards
excerpt: The ability to have serval widgets within a dashboard is incredibly useful.  However one thing that could be a game changer is the creating a math widget that combines other widgets.  For example: one widget shows sales,…

posts.author: Miltime19 (Adam Miller)
posts.cookedHtml: The ability to have serval widgets within a dashboard is incredibly useful. However one thing that could be a game changer is the creating a math widget that combines other widgets. For example: one widget shows sales, the next shows volume, the last one shows how many customers were spoken with. By combining these numbers you could get really useful info such as sales percentage, cash per guest, clients lost percentage. There are hundred of examples to why this would be useful. The formula column is great but is very limiting to the fact you can only use numbers with the same row and that makes getting percentages and averages hard.

posts.author: Qassam-monday.com (Qassam Atme)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Miltime19

Happy you’re loving the dashboards I am thinking about how could we perform your suggestion in-app The widgets import data from boards, so technically it would be natural to make the changes and the calculations on the boards, whereas the widget would just reflect the results (Reporting is the main purpose for widgets and dashboards)
When using the Numbers widgets on formula columns, you can get a sum/average of the whole column. However, was there a function that you cannot achieve by using the Formula and the Numbers widget? I’d love to see whether I can help!

posts.author: Miltime19 (Adam Miller)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, the ones that I listed. Just getting the total columns is limiting. In the formula or total how would you get sales divided by customers? You can take away a number meaning if there are 6 clients with only 5 spots left there’s no way to automate an alert saying there’s only 5 left because the formula only relates to the individual row.

posts.author: Qassam-monday.com (Qassam Atme)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Miltime19 - I am not sure I understand what you are trying to achieve

When you want to combine the widgets in the dashboards, what will the end result be? Can you give me an example?

posts.author: Miltime19 (Adam Miller)
posts.cookedHtml: If you had a widget that showed how many total customers were seen. Then you had another widget that showed how many of those clients purchased ( total sales) . Then the third widget would have total sales widget divided by the total customers widget giving you a closing percentage

posts.author: Qassam-monday.com (Qassam Atme)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Miltime19

I see what you mean! Though I will submit this feature to our product team to consider adding it to our roadmap for Dashboard, in the meantime, let me know if you are looking to accomplish a specific workflow on monday.com. I’d love to help out

posts.author: alexm (Alex M)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 for this feature

In our case, we have:

  • A board with multiple groups, recording sales by type
  • A board recording lost sales

We have a dashboard that has a number widget that sums up all groups to give us the total figure. E.g. Product 1 (group) = £10, Product 2 (group) = £20, therefore total sales = £30. And the same for business lost, for example, total lost = £3.

It would be extremely useful to be able to calculate those two dashboard widgets as a %, so the ‘Lost Business’ widget would report 10%.

percentagewidget1.png1626×1014 122 KB

There isn’t an easy way to do this in boards and it doesn’t justify the mess of an entire board. The ability to calculate %s is so important to reporting.

Thanks guys!

Alex

posts.author: sally (Sally)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bumped. I’m really struggling to do any reporting easily on the dashboards without the ability to take specific columns references and do a basic subtraction, etc.
For example, if I want to track where we are with a budget $X, and invoiced $Y and I want to have a number widget on the dashboard I can grab saying “This is invoiced, and this is underway”, having no ability to do X-Y is super annoying. Formulas in the board aren’t helpful, as they map to all tasks which confuses others who view the board.

Also bumping this, it would still be useful. Especially for budgeting dashboards where we pull info from multiple data sources or boards, it would be very helpful to be able to be able to, in a dashboard widget, display:

posts.author: brianjw (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1. Here’s my use case:

  • A single item on the board represents a single email, of which there are multiple variations (for testing). The versions are each represented as an independent sub-item.
  • The metrics for each version/sub-item (total delivered, unique opens, unique clicks) are input and then summarized onto the main item (SUM)
  • We can use a formula column on the item to give us the Open Rate, Click Rate, Click-to-Open Rate, etc, however on a Dashboard, averaging these rates is not the best practice and it will be off.
  • We need to be able to do the math from the dashboard level so that we can calculate the rates based on the filters on the dashboard at that time - rather than averaging an average.

posts.author: kabelok (Kabelo Komape)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1

In general, not being able to extract sums (a sum in a group, a sum in a board) and use them in calculations/visualisations is a problem that I run into quite a lot. The ability to have the sum of some column in some kind of storage container that can be used throughout a workspace would be such an amazing feature to have

posts.author: kkovriga (Katerina Kovriga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys,

We are happy to announce that we’ve just released the auto-sync from boards feature for our Smart Spreadsheet!

It means that when you import your board to Smart Spreadsheet (as a Dashboard Widget or Board View), all the changes made to the board later are automatically pushed to the spreadsheet. So, your calculations and pivot tables are also updated.

Hope this improvement will make your experience with monday.com more pleasant and smooth.

Best regards,
Katerina Kovriga

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Community, I checked in again with the Dashboards team and they explain that while we are planning to develop formulas within dashboards, it is not currently in development due to other needs and priorities within the dashboards team.

When thinking about the upcoming roadmap, our team needs to make difficult decisions about how we prioritize new features, improve old ones, and ensure overall platform quality.
Although there is no specific timeline or date for when this feature will start development, it is something that the team has in mind for future roadmaps.

As soon as the development teams add this to the official roadmap and starts working on its development, I will update! You can turn on notifications (with the icon on the right side) to make sure you get notified of any changes!

Thanks for your dedication and support.

posts.author: matheusprestes (Matheus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Juliette!
Firstly, thank you for your honest answer.
Please consider what I will say as a project manager. Sometimes we need to take into account the time of an order in the impact and complexity variables. Otherwise, some features will simply never see the light of day. These features may be targeted by other systems in a comparison to discredit your product. I hope I have contributed to the discussion, especially because this topic has already passed or will celebrate its 5th anniversary. Best Regards.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Status update: At the moment the dashboards team is unable to add formulas within dashboards to upcoming roadmap due to other priorities and features already in development.

Our roadmap is subject to change due to evolving capabilities and capacity, so we may be able to add this feature into our future roadmap. In the meantime the option there are some great monday apps mentioned in this thread that can help you with your use case. One suggestion is to use a marketplace app like azyBI Reports and Charts.

As always, your feedback has truly helped our team build future roadmaps, so keep voting and sharing your ideas. If there are any changes in the upcoming months, I will share it on this thread first.

posts.author: arun.paudyal (Arun Paudyal [Decadis AG])
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey monday community,

Arun from Decadis AG here! Great news! We’ve heard your requests and are excited to share a significant step towards meeting your needs. Our latest release of the ‘sumUp for monday’ app now features multi-board support, bringing us closer to your desired functionality.

What does this mean for you? If your monday boards share similar column types, you can now view the sum of those column across all these boards effortlessly through one of our widget in your dashboard. It’s that simple!

Setting it up is a breeze

  1. Connect your boards to the dashboard
  2. Add one of our widgets
  3. Select your preferred column to sum and pivot the data

Eager to see it in action? Check out our screencast for a quick demo!

Alternatively, we’d be thrilled to offer you a free, personalized demo. Just reach out to us using this link.

We’re committed to continuously improving our app based on your feedback. Your pain points are our priorities, and we’re dedicated to creating a tool you’ll love.

Best,
Arun and the Decadis AG team

posts.author: RobaSolutions (Leon from Roba Solutions)

Great news! I’ve developed a solution for this exact problem - a Formula Widget that allows you to perform calculations with dynamic values from different boards and groups.

Key features:

  • List item Combine up to 5 dynamic values from any connected board/group

  • List item Use basic math operations (+, -, *, /)

  • List item Customize the output unit ($, €, %, or your own)

  • List item Simple setup through an intuitive interface

Here’s a quick look at the settings panel:

You can see the widget in action here: https://youtu.be/6Dnd0vubiMY

I’ll keep this thread updated once the app is available on the marketplace!

posts.author: elizabeth.jaeger (Elizabeth)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello @RobaSolutions . I have your app and got the basic formula to work, but it doesn’t seem to be capturing the filters. I have a board with three columns: 1. Borough; 2. Miles of Trails; 3. Miles of Trails Adopted by volunteers. I’m trying to have a widget on the dashboard where I can see a real time snapshot of the % of miles adopted by volunteers by borough at any time. I added the Numbers in the formula for {# of miles adopted} / {# of miles total}, and that works fine. But when I add in the filter to see this % by borough, the % number is unchanged for each borough that I filter. Thoughts?

posts.author: RobaSolutions (Leon from Roba Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Elizabeth,

Thank you for reaching out about your formula widget experience. I understand you’re trying to view the percentage of miles adopted by volunteers for each borough.

Currently, the Formula App uses its own filtering system which is separate from Monday’s dashboard filters. This is why the percentage remains unchanged when you apply dashboard filters by borough.

As a workaround, I’d recommend organizing items with the same borough into groups on your board. This way, you can use the Formula App’s group filtering capability to see the specific calculations for each borough.

I’m actively working on enhancing the Formula App’s functionality to better integrate with Monday’s dashboard filters. I’ll update you as soon as dashboard filter compatibility is available for the Formula App. Your feedback is valuable for these improvements.

Please let me know if you need any assistance implementing the group-based approach or if you have any other questions.

Best regards,
Leon Spitzer

posts.author: RobaSolutions (Leon from Roba Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml: Good news! I’ve fixed the filter issue for Dashboard Formula Widget. It now fully supports Monday’s dashboard filters

posts.author: RobaSolutions (Leon from Roba Solutions)

Dashboost has a lot of features. If you’re just looking for formula calculations and nothing else, Dashboard Formula Widget is a simpler option.

auth.monday.com

Dashboard Formula Widget - monday.com Marketplace

Create formulas with dynamic values from your monday boards

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
RobaSolutions:

Dashboost has a lot of features. If you’re just looking for formula calculations and nothing else, Dashboard Formula Widget is a simpler option.

The Dashboard Formula Widget is definitely useful for basic calculations and aggregations across widgets, especially when you need quick math without exporting data. That said, it still has some limitations when it comes to more advanced logic, conditional calculations, or handling edge cases like unique counts or deduplication across connected boards.

It works well as a lightweight solution, but for more complex reporting needs, you still often have to rely on workarounds, external tools, or API-based solutions. Hopefully we’ll see continued improvements here, because having stronger native formula capabilities at the dashboard level would be a big win.

Appreciate you pointing this out as an option though it’s good to know what’s available today.
